title: Buildprint Mapper OS
creator: Agent Buildprint
summary: Agent-session workflow for mapping an existing repo into evidence-backed, source-independent Agent Buildprint packets.
plainDescription: Use Mapper OS when you already have a working repo and want an agent to turn it into a clean, reusable Buildprint: first map the source, then choose a scoped candidate, then extract an executable packet another agent can implement without reading the original code.
promise: A practical repo-to-Buildprint workflow: evidence-backed discovery, scoped candidate selection, source-independent executable-packet extraction, team/UX/security proof gates, and an honest handover of what is proven, blocked, or intentionally out of scope.
whatYouGet: Repo mapping prompt for Codex, Cursor, Claude Code, or another coding agent
SYSTEM_MAP.md with architecture, flows, data, integrations, tests, side effects, and unknowns
BUILDPRINT_CANDIDATES.md with scoped reusable Buildprint options
A selected executable-packet v2 when you pick a candidate
START_HERE.md, blueprint.yaml, context map, team-stack, UX/design gates, capability packets, proof contracts, and evidence ledger
Clean-room handoff so the implementation agent does not need original source access
No-fake rails: included capabilities must be real, wired, persistent where claimed, and testable
Final handover with evidence inspected, files generated, commands run, blockers, and next direction
whatYouNeed: GitHub/private repo access if the source is private
A human scope choice when multiple candidates exist
Permission to use any private source code
risks: Ugly or static UI without ux-ui-craft and browser proof
checks: If no target repo URL/path/current repo is clear, the agent asks exactly: Which repo URL or local path should I map?
Mapper OS is an agent-session workflow; it does not advertise or require an agb map CLI
Discovery happens before extraction unless the user already selected a scope
Large repos produce scoped candidates before a final package unless full-system mode is explicitly chosen
Generated claims are labeled OBSERVED, INFERRED, QUESTION, or OUT_OF_SCOPE
Secrets and source code are not copied into the generated implementation package
Selected output is source-independent enough for a fresh implementation agent
Selected output uses executable-packet v2: START_HERE.md, blueprint.yaml, 02-context, 03-capabilities, 08-evaluation, and 09-evidence
UI-bearing scopes include ux-contract.md, design-quality-bar.md, and ux-ui-craft routing in team-stack.yaml
Capability packets include product contracts, implementation workflow, source evidence, proof contracts, negative tests, artifacts, and promotion blockers
Mocks/fixtures are not counted as shipped product behavior
Durability, providers, jobs, exports, auth, security, and browser/runtime behavior are real or explicitly blocked/out of scope
Final handover states selected scope, evidence inspected, files generated, commands run, known gaps, and recommended next direction

trustBadges.label: Submission workflow
trustBadges.detail: Defines the official reviewable path from existing repo to scoped Buildprint package.

trustBadges.label: Safety-first extraction
trustBadges.detail: Requires no app-code changes, no secret copying, and explicit unknowns.

trustBadges.label: Agent-session workflow
trustBadges.detail: The mapper runs as coding-agent instructions from the Buildprint package; there is no agb map command.

trustBadges.label: Source-independent output
trustBadges.detail: The generated package must be enough for a fresh implementing agent without original source access.

trustBadges.label: Team-pack gates
trustBadges.detail: Selected outputs route through product-architect, ux-ui-craft, integration/runtime, persistence, security, and verification lenses as applicable.

trustBadges.label: Traceable QA
trustBadges.detail: Requires source evidence to capability contracts to reversal/QA checks instead of generic demo validation.

trustBadges.label: No fake MVPs
trustBadges.detail: Scope may be small, but included capabilities must be real, wired, persistent where relevant, and QA-tested.

trustBadges.label: Fixture review guidance
trustBadges.detail: Fixture projects remain for manual workflow review; they are not an agb map eval harness.

trustBadges.label: Dogfood proof passed
trustBadges.detail: Mapped Seanium/FeedMe with Codex, extracted a static RSS pipeline Buildprint, and passed reversal checks.

howToUse.title: 1. Point it at a repo
howToUse.detail: Open the source repo in your coding agent, or give the agent a repo URL/local path. If the target is unclear, Mapper OS must ask exactly: Which repo URL or local path should I map?

howToUse.title: 2. Bootstrap the Mapper OS package
howToUse.detail: Copy the agent prompt from this page, or run agb start https://agent-buildprint.com/buildprints/buildprint-mapper-os/package.json and follow .buildprint/next-agent.md. Do not use an agb map command.

howToUse.title: 3. Let it discover, not extract yet
howToUse.detail: The agent should inspect the repo and produce SYSTEM_MAP.md plus BUILDPRINT_CANDIDATES.md. For broad repos, this is where it stops and asks you which candidate to extract.

howToUse.title: 4. Choose the scope
howToUse.detail: Pick one product, feature, workflow, or integration. Smaller is fine; vague whole-repo sludge is not. Full-system mode should be explicit.

howToUse.title: 5. Extract the executable packet
howToUse.detail: The selected package should be an executable-packet v2 with START_HERE.md, blueprint.yaml, context/team/UX gates, capability packets, evaluation gates, evidence ledger, and a final handover.

resultChecklist: SYSTEM_MAP.md with source-backed architecture, flows, routes/services, data, integrations, side effects, tests, and unknowns
BUILDPRINT_CANDIDATES.md with scoped candidates and clear included/excluded surfaces
A selected executable-packet v2, not legacy root CAPABILITY_INDEX/TEAM_STACK/UX_CONTRACT sludge
PRE_IMPLEMENTATION_QUESTIONS.md with only blocking questions and safe defaults
02-context/context-map.yaml, team-stack.yaml, ux-contract.md, and design-quality-bar.md where relevant
03-capabilities/* packets with product contract, implementation workflow, source evidence, and proof-contract.yaml
Final handover with selected scope, evidence inspected, files generated, commands run, known gaps, and next recommended action
copyPrompt: Use the Buildprint Mapper OS Buildprint. First bootstrap exact snapshots: agb start https://agent-buildprint.com/buildprints/buildprint-mapper-os/package.json . If agb is not installed, clone https://github.com/DomEscobar/agent-buildprint and run node agent-buildprint/bin/agb.js start https://agent-buildprint.com/buildprints/buildprint-mapper-os/package.json . Then read .buildprint/next-agent.md and continue. Do not write Buildprint snapshots manually. Use it to map a target repo in the current agent session. First resolve the target: if the user has not clearly provided a repository URL, local path, or already-opened current working repo, ask exactly one blocking question: 'Which repo URL or local path should I map?' Do not start discovery until the target is explicit. Do not run agb map; that CLI does not exist. Ask only minimal safety/scope questions before discovery. Then inspect the resolved repo and create SYSTEM_MAP.md and BUILDPRINT_CANDIDATES.md before selected extraction unless I already picked a scope. Ask me to choose one candidate. After selection, extract only that scope into selected-buildprint/ as executable-packet v2 with START_HERE.md, PRE_IMPLEMENTATION_QUESTIONS.md, blueprint.yaml, 02-context/context-map.yaml, 02-context/team-stack.yaml, 03-capabilities/capability-index.yaml, per-capability YAML/proof packets, 08-evaluation gates, and 09-evidence/evidence-ledger.jsonl. If the selected scope has user-facing UI, include 02-context/ux-contract.md, 02-context/design-quality-bar.md, and route ux-ui-craft in 02-context/team-stack.yaml. Infer team packs from product signals; do not ask me to choose a quality tier or team. The selected packet must be source-independent enough for a fresh coding agent to implement without original source access. Default to production-grade selected scope: smaller scope is OK, but do not create a lazy MVP, mock services as product behavior, placeholder routes, no-op controls, skeleton adapters, static UI shells, or in-memory-only persistence when durability is claimed. Do not copy secrets, do not invent validation results, and do not flatten the repo into one vague document. Finish with a chat handover containing outcome, selected scope, evidence inspected, files generated, commands run, known gaps, and recommended next direction.
